section.heading: Former jeopardy

subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.content: No person may, without the person’s consent, be tried by a military commission under this chapter a second time for the same offense.

subsection.heading: Scope of Trial.—
subsection.content: No proceeding in which the accused has been found guilty by military commission under this chapter upon any charge or specification is a trial in the sense of this section until the finding of guilty has become final after review of the case has been fully completed.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 111–84, div. A, title XVIII, § 1802, Oct. 28, 2009, 123 Stat. 2587.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 949h, added Pub. L. 109–366, § 3(a)(1), Oct. 17, 2006, 120 Stat. 2614, related to former jeopardy, prior to the general amendment of this chapter by Pub. L. 111–84.
